chemicalElements.name: Hydrogen
chemicalElements.description: Hydrogen is the lightest element and a common building block in water, acids, and many organic compounds.

chemicalElements.name: Oxygen
chemicalElements.description: Oxygen supports combustion and appears in water, oxides, and many molecules found in air and living systems.

chemicalElements.name: Sodium
chemicalElements.description: Sodium is a soft reactive metal that often forms salts and basic compounds.

chemicalElements.name: Chlorine
chemicalElements.description: Chlorine is a reactive halogen used in salts, disinfectants, and many industrial chemicals.

chemicalElements.name: Carbon
chemicalElements.description: Carbon is the backbone of organic chemistry and can form chains, rings, and many stable compounds.

chemicalElements.name: Nitrogen
chemicalElements.description: Nitrogen makes up most of Earth's atmosphere and appears in ammonia, proteins, and fertilizers.

chemicalElements.name: Sulfur
chemicalElements.description: Sulfur is a yellow nonmetal often found in acids, minerals, and volcanic gases.

chemicalElements.name: Potassium
chemicalElements.description: Potassium is a highly reactive alkali metal important for salts and many biological processes.

chemicalElements.name: Calcium
chemicalElements.description: Calcium is a common metal in minerals and is essential for bones, shells, and cement chemistry.

chemicalElements.name: Magnesium
chemicalElements.description: Magnesium is a light metal used in alloys and forms bright-burning oxides.

chemicalElements.name: Phosphorus
chemicalElements.description: Phosphorus is used in fertilizers, matches, and energy-carrying molecules in living cells.

chemicalElements.name: Iron
chemicalElements.description: Iron is a strong transition metal used in steel and forms several important oxides.

chemicalElements.name: Helium
chemicalElements.description: Helium is a very light noble gas used in balloons, cryogenic systems, and scientific equipment.

chemicalElements.name: Lithium
chemicalElements.description: Lithium is a soft alkali metal best known for its role in rechargeable batteries and lightweight alloys.

chemicalElements.name: Beryllium
chemicalElements.description: Beryllium is a light, stiff metal used in aerospace parts, X-ray windows, and specialized materials.

chemicalElements.name: Boron
chemicalElements.description: Boron is a metalloid used in glass, detergents, heat-resistant materials, and semiconductor applications.

chemicalElements.name: Fluorine
chemicalElements.description: Fluorine is the most reactive halogen and is used in advanced materials, chemical processing, and fluorides.

chemicalElements.name: Neon
chemicalElements.description: Neon is a noble gas famous for its bright glow in signs, lamps, and discharge tubes.

chemicalElements.name: Aluminium
chemicalElements.description: Aluminium is a lightweight, corrosion-resistant metal used in transport, packaging, and construction.

chemicalElements.name: Silicon
chemicalElements.description: Silicon is a metalloid essential to semiconductors, solar cells, glass, and many minerals.

chemicalElements.name: Argon
chemicalElements.description: Argon is an inert noble gas often used to shield welding, lighting systems, and laboratory setups.

chemicalElements.name: Scandium
chemicalElements.description: Scandium is a rare transition metal used in specialty alloys, lamps, and high-performance materials.

chemicalElements.name: Titanium
chemicalElements.description: Titanium is a strong, corrosion-resistant metal used in aircraft, implants, and advanced engineering.

chemicalElements.name: Vanadium
chemicalElements.description: Vanadium is a transition metal used to strengthen steels and in some energy-storage technologies.

chemicalElements.name: Chromium
chemicalElements.description: Chromium is a shiny metal used in stainless steel, protective coatings, and pigments.

chemicalElements.name: Manganese
chemicalElements.description: Manganese is an important alloying metal used in steelmaking, batteries, and chemical processes.

chemicalElements.name: Cobalt
chemicalElements.description: Cobalt is used in magnets, pigments, and rechargeable batteries for electronics and vehicles.

chemicalElements.name: Nickel
chemicalElements.description: Nickel is a corrosion-resistant metal used in alloys, coins, batteries, and plating.

chemicalElements.name: Copper
chemicalElements.description: Copper is an excellent electrical conductor widely used in wiring, plumbing, and alloys.

chemicalElements.name: Zinc
chemicalElements.description: Zinc is used to protect steel from rust, in brass alloys, and in biological systems.

chemicalElements.name: Gallium
chemicalElements.description: Gallium is a soft metal used in electronics, semiconductors, and specialized temperature devices.

chemicalElements.name: Germanium
chemicalElements.description: Germanium is a metalloid used in semiconductors, infrared optics, and electronic components.

chemicalElements.name: Arsenic
chemicalElements.description: Arsenic is a toxic metalloid used in some semiconductors, alloys, and specialized materials.

chemicalElements.name: Selenium
chemicalElements.description: Selenium is a nonmetal used in glassmaking, electronics, solar devices, and trace biology.

chemicalElements.name: Bromine
chemicalElements.description: Bromine is a reactive liquid halogen used in chemical synthesis, flame retardants, and photography.

chemicalElements.name: Krypton
chemicalElements.description: Krypton is a noble gas used in specialty lighting, lasers, and scientific instruments.

chemicalElements.name: Rubidium
chemicalElements.description: Rubidium is a very reactive alkali metal used in research, electronics, and atomic clocks.

chemicalElements.name: Strontium
chemicalElements.description: Strontium is used in fireworks, ceramics, magnets, and some medical imaging materials.

chemicalElements.name: Yttrium
chemicalElements.description: Yttrium is used in phosphors, lasers, superconductors, and high-tech ceramic materials.

chemicalElements.name: Zirconium
chemicalElements.description: Zirconium is a corrosion-resistant metal used in reactors, ceramics, and durable alloys.

chemicalElements.name: Niobium
chemicalElements.description: Niobium is used in superconducting magnets, strong steels, and heat-resistant superalloys.

chemicalElements.name: Molybdenum
chemicalElements.description: Molybdenum strengthens steels and helps materials resist heat, pressure, and corrosion.

chemicalElements.name: Technetium
chemicalElements.description: Technetium is a radioactive transition metal widely known for use in medical imaging.

chemicalElements.name: Ruthenium
chemicalElements.description: Ruthenium is used in electronics, catalysts, and wear-resistant contacts and coatings.

chemicalElements.name: Rhodium
chemicalElements.description: Rhodium is a reflective precious metal used in catalysts, plating, and high-temperature systems.

chemicalElements.name: Palladium
chemicalElements.description: Palladium is a precious metal used in catalysts, electronics, hydrogen storage, and jewelry.

chemicalElements.name: Silver
chemicalElements.description: Silver is a highly conductive precious metal used in electronics, mirrors, and jewelry.

chemicalElements.name: Cadmium
chemicalElements.description: Cadmium is a soft metal used in batteries, pigments, and specialized coatings, though it is toxic.

chemicalElements.name: Indium
chemicalElements.description: Indium is used in touchscreens, semiconductors, solders, and transparent conductive films.

chemicalElements.name: Tin
chemicalElements.description: Tin is used in solders, coatings, and alloys, and it helps protect other metals from corrosion.

chemicalElements.name: Antimony
chemicalElements.description: Antimony is a metalloid used in flame retardants, alloys, semiconductors, and battery materials.

chemicalElements.name: Tellurium
chemicalElements.description: Tellurium is a rare metalloid used in solar cells, alloys, and thermoelectric materials.

chemicalElements.name: Iodine
chemicalElements.description: Iodine is a halogen used in antiseptics, nutrition, imaging chemistry, and laboratory reactions.

chemicalElements.name: Xenon
chemicalElements.description: Xenon is a heavy noble gas used in lamps, ion propulsion, anesthesia research, and detectors.

chemicalElements.name: Cesium
chemicalElements.description: Cesium is a very reactive alkali metal used in atomic clocks, detectors, and vacuum devices.

chemicalElements.name: Barium
chemicalElements.description: Barium is used in drilling fluids, imaging compounds, ceramics, and fireworks.

chemicalElements.name: Lanthanum
chemicalElements.description: Lanthanum is a rare earth metal used in optics, catalysts, batteries, and specialty glass.

chemicalElements.name: Cerium
chemicalElements.description: Cerium is used in catalysts, polishing powders, lighter flints, and rare earth alloys.

chemicalElements.name: Praseodymium
chemicalElements.description: Praseodymium is a rare earth element used in magnets, glasses, and specialty alloys.

chemicalElements.name: Neodymium
chemicalElements.description: Neodymium is best known for powerful permanent magnets used in motors, speakers, and electronics.

chemicalElements.name: Promethium
chemicalElements.description: Promethium is a radioactive rare earth element used mainly in research and specialized devices.

chemicalElements.name: Samarium
chemicalElements.description: Samarium is used in magnets, control rods, lasers, and some medical applications.

chemicalElements.name: Europium
chemicalElements.description: Europium is used in phosphors for screens, lighting, security printing, and luminescent materials.

chemicalElements.name: Gadolinium
chemicalElements.description: Gadolinium is used in MRI contrast agents, magnets, and neutron-absorbing systems.

chemicalElements.name: Terbium
chemicalElements.description: Terbium is used in phosphors, sensors, magnets, and high-tech optical materials.

chemicalElements.name: Dysprosium
chemicalElements.description: Dysprosium is used to improve high-temperature magnets and in data storage technologies.

chemicalElements.name: Holmium
chemicalElements.description: Holmium is used in lasers, magnetic materials, and scientific calibration equipment.

chemicalElements.name: Erbium
chemicalElements.description: Erbium is used in fiber optics, lasers, specialty glass, and communication systems.

chemicalElements.name: Thulium
chemicalElements.description: Thulium is a rare earth element used in portable X-ray devices and specialized lasers.

chemicalElements.name: Ytterbium
chemicalElements.description: Ytterbium is used in lasers, atomic clocks, stress gauges, and advanced alloys.

chemicalElements.name: Lutetium
chemicalElements.description: Lutetium is used in detectors, catalysts, medical imaging, and high-density materials.

chemicalElements.name: Hafnium
chemicalElements.description: Hafnium is used in control rods, superalloys, semiconductors, and heat-resistant systems.

chemicalElements.name: Tantalum
chemicalElements.description: Tantalum is a corrosion-resistant metal used in capacitors, implants, and strong alloys.

chemicalElements.name: Tungsten
chemicalElements.description: Tungsten has an extremely high melting point and is used in tools, filaments, and dense alloys.

chemicalElements.name: Rhenium
chemicalElements.description: Rhenium is a rare metal used in jet engines, catalysts, and very high-temperature alloys.

chemicalElements.name: Osmium
chemicalElements.description: Osmium is a dense precious metal used in hard alloys, electrical contacts, and scientific work.

chemicalElements.name: Iridium
chemicalElements.description: Iridium is a corrosion-resistant metal used in crucibles, spark plugs, and durable electronics.

chemicalElements.name: Platinum
chemicalElements.description: Platinum is a precious metal used in catalysts, jewelry, laboratory tools, and fuel cells.

chemicalElements.name: Gold
chemicalElements.description: Gold is a valuable noble metal used in electronics, jewelry, dentistry, and coatings.

chemicalElements.name: Mercury
chemicalElements.description: Mercury is a liquid metal once used in thermometers, switches, and scientific instruments.

chemicalElements.name: Thallium
chemicalElements.description: Thallium is a soft metal used in electronics, optics, and research, though it is very toxic.

chemicalElements.name: Lead
chemicalElements.description: Lead is a dense metal used in batteries, radiation shielding, and industrial materials.

chemicalElements.name: Bismuth
chemicalElements.description: Bismuth is a heavy metal used in medicines, low-melting alloys, and colorful crystal forms.

chemicalElements.name: Polonium
chemicalElements.description: Polonium is a rare radioactive element used mainly in research and specialized devices.

chemicalElements.name: Astatine
chemicalElements.description: Astatine is a very rare radioactive halogen studied mostly for research and medicine.

chemicalElements.name: Radon
chemicalElements.description: Radon is a radioactive noble gas formed naturally in rocks and soil.

chemicalElements.name: Francium
chemicalElements.description: Francium is an extremely rare radioactive alkali metal known mostly from research.

chemicalElements.name: Radium
chemicalElements.description: Radium is a radioactive alkaline earth metal historically used in luminous materials.

chemicalElements.name: Actinium
chemicalElements.description: Actinium is a radioactive element used in research and as an actinide reference point.

chemicalElements.name: Thorium
chemicalElements.description: Thorium is a radioactive metal studied for fuel cycles, alloys, and high-temperature materials.

chemicalElements.name: Protactinium
chemicalElements.description: Protactinium is a rare radioactive actinide used mainly in research.

chemicalElements.name: Uranium
chemicalElements.description: Uranium is a heavy radioactive metal used in nuclear fuel, science, and some dense materials.

chemicalElements.name: Neptunium
chemicalElements.description: Neptunium is a radioactive actinide produced in reactors and studied in nuclear chemistry.

chemicalElements.name: Plutonium
chemicalElements.description: Plutonium is a radioactive actinide important in reactors, research, and energy systems.

chemicalElements.name: Americium
chemicalElements.description: Americium is a radioactive element known for use in smoke detectors and laboratory work.

chemicalElements.name: Curium
chemicalElements.description: Curium is a radioactive actinide used in research and some specialized power sources.

chemicalElements.name: Berkelium
chemicalElements.description: Berkelium is a synthetic radioactive element used almost entirely for scientific research.

chemicalElements.name: Californium
chemicalElements.description: Californium is a strong neutron emitter used in research, industry, and reactor applications.

chemicalElements.name: Einsteinium
chemicalElements.description: Einsteinium is a synthetic radioactive element produced in very small amounts for research.

chemicalElements.name: Fermium
chemicalElements.description: Fermium is a synthetic radioactive actinide known from laboratory production and study.

chemicalElements.name: Mendelevium
chemicalElements.description: Mendelevium is a synthetic radioactive element created for nuclear research.

chemicalElements.name: Nobelium
chemicalElements.description: Nobelium is a synthetic actinide produced in laboratories for heavy-element research.

chemicalElements.name: Lawrencium
chemicalElements.description: Lawrencium is a synthetic radioactive element at the end of the actinide series.

chemicalElements.name: Rutherfordium
chemicalElements.description: Rutherfordium is a synthetic transition metal known only from laboratory production.

chemicalElements.name: Dubnium
chemicalElements.description: Dubnium is a synthetic element studied in heavy-ion experiments and nuclear chemistry.

chemicalElements.name: Seaborgium
chemicalElements.description: Seaborgium is a synthetic transition metal observed only in particle accelerator research.

chemicalElements.name: Bohrium
chemicalElements.description: Bohrium is a short-lived synthetic element produced for nuclear science experiments.

chemicalElements.name: Hassium
chemicalElements.description: Hassium is a synthetic heavy element known from a small number of laboratory-created atoms.

chemicalElements.name: Meitnerium
chemicalElements.description: Meitnerium is a synthetic element produced in research facilities and studied briefly after creation.

chemicalElements.name: Darmstadtium
chemicalElements.description: Darmstadtium is a synthetic heavy element made in particle accelerators for fundamental research.

chemicalElements.name: Roentgenium
chemicalElements.description: Roentgenium is a synthetic element known from laboratory experiments on superheavy atoms.

chemicalElements.name: Copernicium
chemicalElements.description: Copernicium is a synthetic heavy metal created in laboratories and studied for nuclear properties.

chemicalElements.name: Nihonium
chemicalElements.description: Nihonium is a synthetic superheavy element observed only in a few research experiments.

chemicalElements.name: Flerovium
chemicalElements.description: Flerovium is a synthetic superheavy element produced for studies of atomic stability.

chemicalElements.name: Moscovium
chemicalElements.description: Moscovium is a synthetic element created in particle accelerators and studied for short moments.

chemicalElements.name: Livermorium
chemicalElements.description: Livermorium is a synthetic superheavy element known from specialized nuclear research.

chemicalElements.name: Tennessine
chemicalElements.description: Tennessine is a synthetic heavy halogen-like element produced in only a few experiments.

chemicalElements.name: Oganesson
chemicalElements.description: Oganesson is the heaviest named element and is known only from a handful of synthetic atoms.

recipes.name: Hydrogen
recipes.description: Hydrogen gas is the lightest molecule and an important fuel and industrial reagent.

recipes.name: Oxygen
recipes.description: Oxygen gas is essential for breathing and helps many reactions burn or release energy.

recipes.name: Chlorine
recipes.description: Chlorine gas is a reactive halogen used in disinfectants and chemical manufacturing.

recipes.name: Water
recipes.description: Water is one of the most important compounds for life and covers most of Earth's surface.

recipes.name: Hydrogen Peroxide
recipes.description: Hydrogen peroxide is a reactive liquid often used as a disinfectant and bleaching agent.

recipes.name: Hydrochloric Acid
recipes.description: Hydrochloric acid is a strong acid found in stomach acid and used in many industrial processes.

recipes.name: Salt
recipes.description: Sodium chloride is table salt, a familiar mineral important in food and chemistry.

recipes.name: Sodium Hydroxide
recipes.description: Sodium hydroxide is a strong base widely used in soap making, cleaning, and industry.

recipes.name: Sodium Oxide
recipes.description: Sodium oxide is a basic oxide formed from sodium and oxygen.

recipes.name: Sodium Peroxide
recipes.description: Sodium peroxide is a reactive oxygen-rich compound that can release oxygen in chemical reactions.

recipes.name: Carbon Monoxide
recipes.description: Carbon monoxide is a toxic gas produced by incomplete combustion.

recipes.name: Carbon Dioxide
recipes.description: Carbon dioxide is a common gas in air and a major product of respiration and combustion.

recipes.name: Methane
recipes.description: Methane is the main component of natural gas and a simple fuel molecule.

recipes.name: Carbonic Acid
recipes.description: Carbonic acid forms when carbon dioxide dissolves in water and helps make drinks fizzy.

recipes.name: Ammonia
recipes.description: Ammonia is a pungent gas used in fertilizers, cleaning products, and industrial chemistry.

recipes.name: Nitric Oxide
recipes.description: Nitric oxide is a reactive gas with roles in chemistry and biology.

recipes.name: Nitrogen Dioxide
recipes.description: Nitrogen dioxide is a brown reactive gas often linked to air pollution.

recipes.name: Sulfur Dioxide
recipes.description: Sulfur dioxide is a sharp-smelling gas produced in volcanic activity and fuel burning.

recipes.name: Sulfur Trioxide
recipes.description: Sulfur trioxide is a highly reactive sulfur oxide important in acid production.

recipes.name: Potassium Chloride
recipes.description: Potassium chloride is a common salt used in fertilizers and chemistry.

recipes.name: Calcium Oxide
recipes.description: Calcium oxide, also called quicklime, is important in cement and industrial processes.

recipes.name: Iron(II) Oxide
recipes.description: Iron(II) oxide is a simple iron oxide used here as an early step before the larger Fe2O3 pattern.

recipes.name: Magnesium Oxide
recipes.description: Magnesium oxide is a stable oxide used in refractory materials and chemistry.

recipes.name: Phosphine
recipes.description: Phosphine is a toxic phosphorus-containing gas used in specialized chemistry.

recipes.name: Iron(III) Oxide
recipes.description: Iron(III) oxide is a common iron oxide found in rust and mineral pigments.

themes.name: Basic
themes.description: Learn the core compounds step by step. New elements unlock only when the previous task is complete.
